context.projectOverview: Ports the Claude Agent SDK agent-loop capabilities into agent-bober's own provider-agnostic loop as additive, default-off changes. Sprints 1-4 implement the four architecture-backed high-value gaps (refusal detection, cost substrate + USD budget ceiling, per-role effort, parallel read-only tools); sprints 5-10 implement the remaining capability areas (loop events + hooks, sessions/resume/fork, in-context compaction, streaming deltas, mid-turn interrupt, in-process subagents + opt-in MCP bridge) in the own-loop idiom per explicit user direction, bound by the same hard constraints.

completedSprints.title: Loop wiring
completedSprints.summary: b9c936c: AgenticLoopParams {effort?, budget?}; Budget shared-instance semantics; budgetFromMaxUsd.

completedSprints.title: Parallel tools
completedSprints.summary: 4ab7040: ToolDef.readOnly (bridged MCP tools must stay UNMARKED -> serial per ADR-2).

completedSprints.title: Events + hooks
completedSprints.summary: d52f94c: finish() single exit; LoopEvent union complete.

completedSprints.title: Sessions
completedSprints.summary: c51b28b: session? param — child loops get NO session by default (fresh context).

completedSprints.title: Abort
completedSprints.summary: 2f3636e: abortSignal — consider threading parent's signal into child loops (recommend YES: pass parentParams.abortSignal so aborting the parent stops children; document the decision). agentic-loop.ts ~850 lines; runAgenticLoop is a pure function over params (re-entrant, nesting-safe). MCP handshake/tools-call pattern lives in src/graph/client.ts (fixed 2026-06-20: initialize + tools/call). Suite 3870.

contract.title: In-process scoped subagents (spawn_subagent tool) + opt-in MCP tool bridge
contract.description: The last two capability areas, both opt-in. (1) Subagents: AgenticLoopParams.subagents registers a spawn_subagent tool; its handler runs a nested runAgenticLoop with a fresh context, a scoped tool subset, optional per-agent model/effort/maxTurns, charging the SAME parent Budget, and returns the child's finalText to the parent as a tool result — the parent's context grows by the summary only. (2) MCP bridge: an off-by-default config axis exposes a configured MCP server's tools as loop ToolDefs routed through a bridge using the repo's existing MCP client transport; disabled (default) spawns nothing and changes nothing, preserving loop hermeticity.

successCriteria.description: With subagents configured, the parent loop exposes a spawn_subagent tool; invoking it runs a nested loop whose client sees ONLY the subagent's scoped tools and a fresh message history (no parent turns), and the child's finalText comes back as the parent's tool result.
The nested loop honors per-agent model, effort and maxTurns overrides and charges tokens/costUsd into the parent's Budget instance (combined spend visible; a child cannot out-spend a parent ceiling).
A child that refuses, exceeds budget, or errors surfaces as an isError tool result to the parent (loop continues; parent decides), never a throw.

Without a subagents param the spawn_subagent tool is not registered and the tool list is byte-identical; with the MCP bridge disabled (default) no MCP process/transport is created and the tool list is unchanged.

With the bridge enabled in config, the configured MCP server's tools appear as ToolDefs (unmarked readOnly → serial execution), calls route through the bridge to the MCP transport, and close() tears the connection down cleanly at loop end.
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not allow recursive subagent nesting deeper than one level (child loops get no subagents param — hard cap, mirrors the Workflow one-level rule).
Do not register the MCP bridge on the hermetic claude-code child path or fleet children (--strict-mcp-config stays).
Do not mark bridged MCP tools readOnly (unknown side effects → serial per ADR-2's conservative default).
Do not build persona-file (.md) loading into subagent defs — SubagentDef is a programmatic surface; the existing agent-loader personas remain the role layer.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: scoped fresh-context spawn with summary-only return, shared-Budget accounting with overrides, fail-soft child outcomes, double off-by-default (no tool, no MCP spawn), and bridged round-trip with clean close.
contract.definitionOfDone: A parent loop can delegate a bounded subtask to a fresh-context nested loop with scoped tools and per-agent model/effort under the parent's budget, and a config-enabled MCP bridge can extend the tool catalog through the existing MCP client — while both features absent leave every byte of current behavior unchanged. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: runAgenticLoop is re-entrant (pure function over its params) so nesting is safe; the child gets its own params object and shares only the Budget reference.
The repo already has an MCP client transport (src/graph/client.ts MCP initialize + tools/call pattern, fixed 2026-06-20) the bridge can reuse or mirror — no new MCP dependency.

The bridge config lives as an optional section (e.g. config.tools.mcpBridge { enabled: false, server: { command, args } }) following the egress-axis default-off idiom used by medical/research sections.
SubagentDef = { name, description, systemPrompt, tools: string[], model?, effort?, maxTurns? } — an own type; per-agent effort rides ChatParams.effort from sprint 3 (hard dependsOn).
spawn_subagent's input schema is { name: string, task: string } — the model picks a configured subagent by name and passes the task text.
outOfScope: Parallel subagent fan-out (one spawn per tool call; the fleet layer owns bulk fan-out).
MCP server lifecycle management beyond start/close for a single configured server.
Exposing subagents or the bridge in bober.config.json role sections for pipeline roles (a consumer wires them explicitly; config surface here is the bridge axis only).
contract.generatorNotes: Subagents: create src/orchestrator/subagents.ts exporting SubagentDef and buildSubagentTool(defs, parentParams): { tool: ToolDef, handler: ToolHandler }. The handler: resolve def by name (unknown name → isError result listing valid names); build child AgenticLoopParams { client: parentParams.client (or a model-overridden client via the provider factory when def.model set — reuse how roles resolve clients), systemPrompt: def.systemPrompt, userMessage: task, tools: parentParams.tools filtered to def.tools, toolHandlers filtered likewise, maxTurns: def.maxTurns ?? 10, effort: def.effort, budget: parentParams.budget (SAME instance), subagents: undefined }; await runAgenticLoop; map result → tool result text (finalText, or isError for refused/budget_exceeded/error with the stopReason named). In agentic-loop.ts (or the entry assembling tools), when params.subagents?.length, append the built tool/handler before the loop starts. MCP bridge: create src/orchestrator/tools/mcp-bridge.ts exporting createMcpToolBridge(server: {command, args}): Promise<{ tools: ToolDef[], handlers: Map<string,ToolHandler>, close(): Promise<void> }> — initialize over stdio using the same MCP handshake/tool-call encoding as src/graph/client.ts (tools/list → ToolDefs with a 'mcp__' name prefix to avoid collisions; tools/call → result text). Config: optional ToolsSectionSchema { mcpBridge?: { enabled: z.boolean().default(false), server: {...} } } — but ONLY construct the bridge when enabled === true at the call site (never at config parse). Ensure close() runs in a finally at loop end. Inject the transport for tests (accept an optional transport factory) so no real process spawn is needed.
contract.evaluatorNotes: sc-10-1: parent fake client scripts a spawn_subagent tool call; child fake client (or shared scripted client distinguishing by system prompt) asserts the child's first request contains ONLY scoped tool defs and no parent messages; parent's next turn receives the child finalText as tool result. sc-10-2: shared Budget — assert usdSpent/tokensSpent include child turns; set a ceiling the child crosses → child stops with budget_exceeded and parent sees isError naming it (also covers sc-10-3 partially). sc-10-3: child scripted refusal → parent isError mentioning refusal; loop continues. sc-10-4: no subagents → tool defs deep-equal baseline; default config → assert injected transport factory never called. sc-10-5: stub transport implementing initialize/tools-list/tools-call; assert bridged ToolDefs appear prefixed, a call round-trips, readOnly is absent on them, and close() called once at loop end (finally). Build + typecheck + FULL suite.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
